name: google-calendar-mcp
title: Google Calendar
description: Google Calendar via REST API — events, calendars, freebusy, create/update/delete
tagline: Google Calendar events and scheduling via REST API
author: community

highlights.text: Events — list, create, update, delete calendar events
Search — query events by keyword, date range, and calendar
Free/busy — check availability across multiple calendars
Multi-calendar — manage events across all Google calendars
OAuth 2.0 — standard Google consent flow, no stored passwords

readme:
### What it does

Google Calendar MCP connects your AI agent to Google Calendar through the official REST API. It provides tools for listing calendars, querying events by date range, creating and updating events, deleting events, and checking free/busy availability.

Your agent can check today's schedule, find open meeting slots, create new events with attendees, and manage calendar entries — all through a standard Google OAuth consent flow.

### Community plugin

This is a curated community plugin maintained by Anthropic. It implements the `calendar-v1` service contract, making it interchangeable with the CalDAV Blade for Google Calendar users who prefer the native REST API over CalDAV protocol access.

### Setup

Setup requires a Google Cloud project with the Calendar API enabled and OAuth 2.0 credentials (Desktop app type). On first run, the plugin opens a browser for the standard Google consent flow. Subsequent sessions use the stored refresh token.

### Comparison with CalDAV Blade

| Feature | Google Calendar MCP | CalDAV Blade |
|---------|--------------------|--------------|
| Protocol | Google REST API | CalDAV (RFC 4791) |
| Auth | OAuth 2.0 | Username + password |
| Providers | Google only | Any CalDAV server |
| Setup | Moderate (Cloud project) | Simple (URL + credentials) |

setup.help_text: Create a Google Cloud project, enable the Calendar API, create OAuth 2.0 credentials, and complete the consent flow on first run.
prerequisites: Google Cloud project with Calendar API enabled

scenarios.label: Check availability and schedule a meeting
scenarios.body: Query free/busy across attendee calendars, find the next open slot that works for everyone, and create the event with a title, location, and description.

scenarios.label: Review and clean up the week ahead
scenarios.body: List all events for the coming week, identify conflicts or back-to-back meetings, and suggest adjustments or cancellations to create focus-time blocks.

notes: Curated third-party plugin. Not yet conformance-tested against calendar-v1. Would trigger calendar v2 adapter work if activated alongside CalDAV.
